sections.paragraph:
m.-(I) a river, a moist country, Jer 17:8. Root יָבַל No. I. 1.

(II) pr.n. Jubal, son of Lamech, inventor of music, Gen 4:21. Perhaps as an appellative Jubal signified jubilum, or the sound and noise of the trumpet and other instruments, music, (kindred with יֹובֵל), and thus it was afterwards applied to the inventor. [As if the Scripture account were not to be simply believed.] As to the conjecture of Buttman (Mythologus, i. 163, seq. 169), that the name of Apollo comes from the same source, I express no opinion.
